description_long: This is a traditional Japanese-style stone lantern, known as a tōrō, featuring a robust, square base that supports a tapered pedestal. The central lantern chamber is enclosed by a grid-patterned window, through which a warm, amber light glows, suggesting an internal light source. The roof is broad and gently flared, with a slightly upturned edge and a central finial, giving the lantern a pagoda-like silhouette. The surface appears to be made of dark, textured stone, lending it a sense of permanence and durability. The design is minimalist, with no visible decorative engravings or embellishments, focusing on geometric symmetry and clean lines. This lantern would typically be found in parks, gardens, or along pathways, providing both illumination and an ornamental focal point.
description: A dark stone Japanese-style lantern with a grid-patterned window and a glowing amber light, featuring a pagoda-like roof and sturdy pedestal base.
description_view_0: Front view showing the grid-patterned window with warm light and the symmetrical, flared roof.
description_view_1: Side view nearly identical to the front, highlighting the lantern's depth and the thickness of the stone structure.
description_view_2: Rear view, similar to the front, with the same grid window and roof details visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, mirroring the features of the other side, with the same illuminated window and robust base.
